_comment: Phrases the tripwire checker bans from generated copy. Each entry is matched against every slide body and the caption. A single match fails the run. Replace or extend these categories for your domain.

categories.description: Language that implies guaranteed investment returns, risk-free outcomes, or directional market predictions. Required for any brand publishing financial or investment-adjacent content.
categories.rationale: Regulatory risk under SEC and FCA guidelines. Any implication of guaranteed returns or risk-free outcomes constitutes unlicensed investment advice. Even casual phrasing like 'will outperform' can trigger compliance review. The cost of a false positive (rewriting one slide) is zero compared to the cost of a compliance incident.

categories.description: Health outcome claims that lack attribution to a named source. Catches copy that implies causation without a cited study or clinical body.
categories.rationale: FTC guidelines require that health claims in marketing be substantiated and attributed. 'Studies show' without a named source is unsubstantiated. The pipeline sanitizes source items to eliminate this at input, but the model can re-introduce it from training data. This tripwire is the last line of defense.

_match_types_comment: Each phrase has a 'match' field: 'literal' for exact substring (case-insensitive by default), or 'regex' for pattern matching with optional 'flags'. The checker is intentionally aggressive — false positives are cheap, false negatives are expensive.
_seeding_comment: When seeding this file, write the rationale BEFORE the phrases. If you can't articulate why a phrase is banned, you'll struggle to defend the ban later, and you'll be tempted to remove it the first time it fires on copy you wanted to ship.
